game_description: Capture the enemy flag through deduction and deception, 2 players.

strategies.condition: Placing your flag at game start
strategies.strategy_detailed: Place your flag in a corner with 2-3 Bombs directly adjacent. Surround it with at least one Miner adjacent (to trap opponent Miners you send back). The classic back-corner trap is well-known — consider placing the flag one row off the back edge with bombs behind it for surprise.
strategy_stealth: 1 Miner near flag as reverse trap
Flag 1 row up, bombs behind = surprise

strategies.strategy_detailed: Keep your Marshal (10) and General (9) away from the front line initially. Use Colonels (8) and Majors (7) as your front-line probers. Your Marshal is a one-time-use weapon — deploy it only to eliminate opponent's identified Marshal or General, not to probe.

Scatter 2-3 Scouts (rank 2) near the front but keep them movable — they're your primary information gatherers. One Scout at a lake gap can probe across the board. Never cluster all Scouts: any rank 3+ piece (Miner, Sergeant, anything higher) picks clustered Scouts off one by one.

Probe with Sergeants (4) and Lieutenants (5) — affordable losses with high info gain. Each combat reveals BOTH ranks (your probe AND opponent's piece). Even losing narrows their flag's location. Never probe with Marshal (10) or General (9) — revealing them gives away your decisive pieces.

Keep your Marshal stationary behind your lines for the first 5-7 moves. An early-moving powerful piece advertises itself. Once your opponent has committed their Marshal to the front, reveal yours for the decisive strike.
strategy_stealth: Reveal only after opponent commits theirs

strategies.strategy_detailed: If your Marshal is identified, use it as a known threat rather than a surprise weapon. Push it forward to control corridors while your Spy (only 1 per side) flanks toward the enemy's likely Marshal. Your General and Colonel become the true surprise — opponent fixates on the visible Marshal.

When you've narrowed the flag to a 2-3 square area surrounded by bombs, send Miners directly. Route Miners through your most protected corridor. Keep 2 Miners in reserve — one to reach the flag area, one to absorb a potential decoy bomb.
strategy_stealth: Send Miners when flag zone identified

strategies.strategy_detailed: Stationary pieces are Bombs or the Flag. Mark all pieces that have never moved — these are your target set. Among those, the ones with adjacent bomb-like stationary pieces are highest-probability flag locations. Mentally update this map every turn.

The Spy beats the Marshal ONLY when Spy attacks first — if Marshal attacks your Spy, Marshal wins. So move Spy adjacent then strike, never let it sit defensively next to the Marshal. Any Scout (rank 2) or higher that intercepts kills the Spy outright. Clear the corridor first.
strategy_stealth: Spy beats Marshal ONLY on attack

strategies.strategy_detailed: In endgame with a locked opponent back row, Miners are your only winning pieces. Protect remaining Miners aggressively. If you have 2+ Miners and opponent has 4+ bombs, use one Miner as bait toward a decoy area while routing the real strike from the opposite flank.
strategy_stealth: Miners = only pieces that win now

strategies.condition: Few pieces remaining on each side
strategies.strategy_detailed: With fewer than 8 pieces per side, reconstruct the full board state from memory. You likely know the rank of every remaining opponent piece through previous battles. Use this knowledge to route your Miners around high-rank pieces and funnel them into bombs.
strategy_stealth: All ranks should be known by end

strategies.strategy_detailed: A still-hidden Marshal in endgame is your most powerful asset. Reveal it only to eliminate the opponent's last meaningful threat. Use it to clear a corridor for your Miners — that's its final purpose. Don't waste it on low-rank pieces when Miners need a path.
